$description: Multi-script visual language: how the design adapts across scripts and locales while maintaining contemplative quality. This file owns the visual rules — font stacks, line-height adjustments, loading strategy, and fallback marking. Portal-specific concerns (search behavior, URL routing, chunk relations, language priorities) belong in the portal's architecture. Source: DES-017, PRI-06.

per-script-typography.$description: Font stacks for non-Latin scripts. Each script has a reading and UI font from the Noto family. Loading strategy is locale-gated.

devanagari.loading: Hindi locale (/hi/) eagerly preloads both. English pages load conditionally when Devanagari characters present.
devanagari.line-height-adjustment: Devanagari requires 1.9–2.0 line-height (taller glyphs with hanging characters)

thai.note: Thai script has no whitespace between words — affects text measure calculations

per-script-typography.font-loading-principle: Non-Latin fonts load only when needed. unicode-range gating prevents unnecessary downloads. System font fallbacks with size-adjust provide instant rendering.

fallback-marking.$description: When content appears in a language different from the seeker's locale, the design language marks it honestly. The visual treatment is the same regardless of which feature triggered the fallback.

visual-treatment.tag: [EN] — small, muted, inline. Uses text-secondary color at ambient opacity.
visual-treatment.link: 'Read in English' — standard gold interactive link, not a button or CTA.
visual-treatment.tone: Honest, not apologetic. Absence of translated content is treated as an invitation, not a failure.

fallback-marking.design-stance: The [EN] tag is warm and quiet. It says 'this is available in English' the way a librarian says 'we have that on the other shelf.' No red badges, no warning icons, no 'sorry' copy.

cultural-visual-adaptation.$description: Some visual elements require cultural adaptation beyond mechanical translation. These are design-level decisions, not content decisions.
cultural-visual-adaptation.seeking-entry-points: The 'Seeking...' entry points are deeply English-idiomatic ('The heart to forgive'). Each locale's reviewer may rephrase, reorder, or replace entirely. These are editorial content per locale, not translated strings.
cultural-visual-adaptation.practice-bridge: The practice bridge annotation (see patterns/reading-surface.pattern.json) is localized and SRF-reviewed in all locales. The visual treatment stays identical; only the copy changes.
